name: devops-troubleshooter
description: Expert DevOps troubleshooter specializing in rapid incident
capabilities: Diagnose Kubernetes pod failures, OOMKills, and resource constraint issues
Analyze logs from ELK Stack, Loki, and cloud-native observability platforms
Debug distributed tracing issues across microservices with Jaeger, Zipkin, and OpenTelemetry
Troubleshoot network latency, DNS resolution, and load balancer problems
Investigate CI/CD pipeline failures and implement automated debugging workflows
Perform root cause analysis for database deadlocks and performance bottlenecks
author: sickn33
securitySummary: Prompt-only skill with no executable code. Static analysis scanned 0 files (0 lines) and detected 0 security issues. This is a pure text-based skill that provides debugging guidance through structured prompts. No risk factors present.
valueStatement: Rapidly diagnose and resolve production incidents with AI-guided debugging, log analysis, and systematic troubleshooting methodologies.
limitations: Cannot directly access external systems, logs, or monitoring dashboards
Cannot execute commands or scripts on infrastructure
Requires user to provide logs, metrics, and system context for accurate diagnosis
Cannot replace human judgment for critical production decisions

useCases.targetUser: Site Reliability Engineers and DevOps Engineers
useCases.title: Production Incident Response
useCases.description: Get immediate guidance for diagnosing and resolving production outages, from initial assessment through root cause analysis.

useCases.title: Kubernetes Cluster Debugging
useCases.description: Troubleshoot pod failures, networking issues, service mesh problems, and resource constraints in Kubernetes environments.

useCases.title: Performance Optimization
useCases.description: Identify performance bottlenecks through log analysis, distributed tracing correlation, and system profiling recommendations.

promptTemplates.title: Basic Incident Triage
promptTemplates.prompt: My production service is experiencing [describe issue: high latency/errors/outage]. I have [describe available data: logs from X, metrics from Y]. Help me diagnose the root cause.
promptTemplates.scenario: Initial incident response with limited information

promptTemplates.title: Kubernetes Pod Debugging
promptTemplates.prompt: I have a Kubernetes pod in [CrashLoopBackOff/Running state] with the following events: [paste kubectl describe output]. The logs show: [paste relevant logs]. What should I investigate?

promptTemplates.title: Log Analysis Request
promptTemplates.prompt: I'm seeing this error pattern in my [ELK/Loki/cloud logs]: [paste error messages and timestamps]. The error started [when]. Help me correlate these logs and identify the root cause.
promptTemplates.scenario: Analyzing log patterns to identify issues

promptTemplates.title: Post-Mortem Analysis
promptTemplates.prompt: We had an incident where [describe incident]. Timeline: [paste timeline]. The following systems were affected: [list]. What systemic issues contributed to this failure and how can we prevent recurrence?

bestPractices: Always gather logs, metrics, and system state before forming hypotheses to avoid misdiagnosis
Start with the simplest explanation and escalate to complex causes only when ruled out
Document all investigation steps and findings for postmortem and knowledge sharing
antiPatterns: Making changes to production systems without first reproducing the issue in a controlled environment
Ignoring error messages and symptoms that seem unrelated to the primary issue
Focusing on symptoms rather than root cause, leading to temporary fixes that fail later

faq.question: Can this skill execute commands on my servers?
faq.answer: No. This is a prompt-based skill that provides guidance and recommendations. You must execute any commands yourself.

faq.question: What observability platforms does this skill support?
faq.answer: It supports ELK Stack, Loki/Grafana, DataDog, New Relic, Jaeger, Zipkin, Prometheus, and OpenTelemetry among others.

faq.question: Can I use this skill for security incident response?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill includes guidance for authentication debugging, certificate issues, and security log analysis.

faq.question: Does this skill work with cloud platforms like AWS and Azure?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers AWS CloudWatch, Azure Monitor, GCP Cloud Logging, and multi-cloud troubleshooting scenarios.

faq.question: How does this skill handle Kubernetes debugging?
faq.answer: It provides guidance for kubectl commands, pod troubleshooting, service mesh issues, networking, and storage problems.

faq.question: Can this skill help with CI/CD pipeline issues?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers build failures, deployment problems, GitOps issues, and pipeline optimization.
